meaning: adj.印象派的,印象主义的,给人深刻印象的
examples: His paintings had become more impressionistic as his eyesight dimmed.
The effect is impressionistic and stunning.
The mood shifts from playful and acrobatic to brooding and impressionistic in the following tunes.
Vine describes itself as a sort of video picture postcard, an impressionistic document of a moment.
